description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1014 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0377#AN1061 . The description of the approach follows: Unauthorized or anomalous loading of kernel-mode drivers or DLLs, concealed services, or abnormal modification of boot components indicative of rootkit activity.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Detection Strategy DET0377: Rootkit Activity

items.content: Deploy and configure a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system to aggregate logs from Windows endpoints, domain controllers, and network devices.
Enable and configure Windows Event Logging to capture Security, System, and Application logs with a focus on Event ID 7045 (Service Installation) and Event ID 4697 (Service Creation).
Implement Sysmon with a configuration that logs driver loads (Event ID 6), image loads (Event ID 7), and file creation (Event ID 11) with enhanced detail.
Utilize Windows Defender Antivirus or a compatible EDR solution to monitor and alert on known rootkit signatures and behaviors.
Profile typical driver loads, service installations, and boot configuration changes during normal operations.
Create whitelists of authorized kernel drivers, services, and boot components.
Configure alerts for deviations from the established baseline, such as unsigned drivers or services with concealed attributes.
Establish baseline monitoring for normal system activity to identify anomalies.
Monitor for attempts to disable or tamper with security logging, antivirus, or EDR services, which may precede rootkit installation.
Conduct regular integrity checks on critical system files and boot sectors using tools like Windows File Integrity Checker (sfc) or third-party solutions.
Correlate alerts from multiple sources (SIEM, EDR, AV) to confirm rootkit activity.
Isolate affected systems from the network to prevent lateral movement or data exfiltration.
Collect and analyze memory dumps, disk images, and relevant logs for forensic evidence.
Use specialized rootkit detection tools (e.g., GMER, RootkitRevealer) for deep analysis.
Remove malicious components, restore systems from clean backups, and patch vulnerabilities.
Investigate and respond to detected anomalies.
